items.title: CI: stop running pull-request code on the persistent self-hosted runner
items.story: Critical audit finding: public PR code runs on a self-hosted runner that later holds the model key (ci.yml self-hosted + checkout PR content + npm ci + docker build + sudo + E2E_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in one job). Run pull_request validation on GitHub-hosted or truly ephemeral one-job runners; move the real-model turn to a separate workflow gated on a reviewed trusted SHA / protected environment; do not expose a persistent host Docker daemon to PR code; minimize GITHUB_TOKEN permissions.
acceptance_criteria: pull_request jobs no longer run on a persistent self-hosted runner (GitHub-hosted or destroyed-after-run ephemeral)
the secret-bearing real-model step runs only from a trusted SHA / protected environment, not from PR-controlled code
GITHUB_TOKEN permissions minimized; no persistent host Docker daemon exposed to PR code
authorized canary PR proves PR code cannot persist a marker/process/binary/Docker object observable by a later trusted job

items.title: Inbound attachments: one atomic O_NOFOLLOW writer for Telegram + Slack
items.story: High audit finding: Telegram (fs.writeFile / open(path,'w')) and Slack (lstat-then-open race) attachment writers can follow a symlink out of the workspace and truncate a service-account-writable file. Create one shared hardened writer: O_CREAT|O_EXCL|O_NOFOLLOW into a verified attachment dir, validate the descriptor + containment, stream in, then atomically publish without replacing an existing target (or use generated immutable storage names + original name as metadata). Reuse the descriptor-containment pattern already in workspace-message-attachment.ts (currently a reader).
acceptance_criteria: every inbound channel attachment write goes through one shared O_NOFOLLOW descriptor-based writer
real-filesystem adversarial tests: existing final-file symlink, final-file swap during open, ancestor swap, hard links, streaming + buffered paths; the outside target is never truncated or unlinked
no check-then-open race remains (atomic create-if-absent)

items.title: LLM passthrough: race-free concurrency admission + fewer body copies
items.story: High audit finding: /llm/v1/* has a per-minute counter but no concurrency semaphore, and buffers ≤16 MiB bodies several times (public read -> parse -> reserialize -> loopback -> gateway read). Acquire a race-free global and per-app/key concurrency permit BEFORE reading the body; reject excess before consuming bodies; release in one finally covering disconnect/parse/setup/provider failures. Reduce the body limit unless 16 MiB is justified. (Loopback collapse is the separate SIMP-3.)
acceptance_criteria: a global and per-app/key concurrency permit is acquired before body read; excess requests are rejected before body consumption
the permit is released in a single finally covering disconnect, parse failure, setup failure, provider failure, and streamed cleanup
barrier test: with upstream held, more-than-cap requests prove only the allowed number begin body consumption; RSS and open sockets measured under concurrent max-size requests

items.title: IPC replay: move marker cleanup off the per-request admission path
items.story: High audit finding: reserveFreshIpcRequestId runs pruneConsumedIpcRequestIds first, which readdirSync + read/parse every replay marker on EVERY signed IPC request (~O(rate^2) over the 5-min window). Remove cleanup from admission; use periodic/amortized bounded cleanup, expiry-bucketed dirs, or a durable-uniqueness store with TTL. Preserve atomic create-if-absent replay protection; do a constant/tightly-bounded amount of work per request; avoid synchronous directory-wide I/O on the event loop.
acceptance_criteria: no synchronous directory-wide scan on the request admission path
prepopulate 5k and 20k markers; one validation performs a bounded number of FS ops with stable latency
replay rejection after process restart and concurrent duplicate reservation still hold

items.title: Jobs: batch the latest-run projection (kill the list N+1)
items.story: High audit finding: GET /v1/jobs runs loadLatestRunsByJobId = Promise.all of one listJobRuns(job.id,1) per job (up to 500 concurrent). Add one batch repository method returning the latest run for all requested job IDs via DISTINCT ON (job_id) or a window function; build the visibility metadata synchronously from two maps. Subsumes the audit's simplification #4.
acceptance_criteria: a 500-job list issues a constant, small number of SQL queries (instrumented)
latest-run ordering, health/staleness, error summaries, app filtering, and the public response schema are unchanged
load test: concurrent list requests hold pool wait time and p95/p99 within target

items.title: SSE: reserve the stream permit before subscription setup
items.story: Medium audit finding (TOCTOU): the session event-stream cap is checked before activeStreams is incremented after an await, so >25 concurrent setups all pass. Reserve/increment a token synchronously immediately after the check, before the first await; release it in one idempotent cleanup on setup failure/disconnect/close/normal end (mirror the sibling wait route). NOTE: overlaps CO-2 (coordination hardening TOCTOU batch) — land it there or here, not twice.
acceptance_criteria: the stream permit is reserved synchronously before the first await
barrier test: 26 concurrent requests admit exactly 25; the rest get 429 TOO_MANY_STREAMS; permits returned once on failure/disconnect
reconciled with CO-2 so the TOCTOU fix is not duplicated

items.title: Rate limits: cluster-authoritative store or declared singleton (decision required)
items.story: Medium audit finding: control + gateway rate limiters are per-process in-memory Maps, so the effective limit multiplies by replica count and resets on restart. Architecture decision required: enforce counters through one shared atomic store (Postgres/Redis) OR make the service singleton and reject horizontally-scaled config; document per-process vs cluster-wide for each limit. Record a decision before implementing.
acceptance_criteria: a decision record chooses shared-store vs declared-singleton and is accepted
two live replicas accept a combined count equal to the configured cluster limit (if shared-store) OR horizontally-scaled config is rejected (if singleton)
each limit documented as per-process or cluster-wide

items.title: Live-admission: admission caps + terminal retention (decision required)
items.story: Medium audit finding: the durable live-admission table has no per-app/per-conversation admission cap at insert and no terminal retention (rows with identity metadata accumulate forever). Add atomic admission limits at the insert transaction returning an explicit overloaded/deferred outcome; define terminal retention with an ended_at index and a bounded purge/archival job. Architecture decision required and its retention half overlaps DUR-1 / deferral D-0001 — reconcile so retention is defined once.
acceptance_criteria: atomic per-app (and optionally per-conversation) admission cap; overload returns an explicit outcome; exact replay still returns the existing item
terminal retention defined with an ended_at-based index and a bounded purge that never removes active/deferred/recently-completed items
retention ownership reconciled with DUR-1 / D-0001 (defined once)

items.title: Make check:architecture a required CI gate
items.story: Medium audit finding: check:architecture is a documented ratchet but absent from CI. PAY-1 already restored a green baseline (exit 0). Add the command as a required CI step; add a fixture/controlled mutation per rule class proving CI fails on one additional violation; baseline any accepted debt with exact path/count entries. Sequence with E2E-3 (the same make-a-green-checker-required move).
acceptance_criteria: check:architecture runs in ci.yml and a clean HEAD passes it
a controlled mutation per rule class proves CI fails on one added violation
expired/over-budget exceptions fail the gate

items.title: One canonical source for runtime queue defaults
items.story: Audit simplification: queue defaults are encoded three times (settings-defaults, settings-parser, group-queue-policy). Export one immutable RuntimeQueueSettings default; parse by overlaying validated input onto it; pass resolved queue settings into GroupQueue. Preserve values, 0=unlimited, validation ranges, host-capacity clamping, YAML rendering, workstation defaults.
acceptance_criteria: one canonical default; parseSettings({}) equals it; round-trips through renderer/parser
duplicate parser/queue-policy constants removed; no config/runtime circular dependency
boundary tests for zero, negative, oversized values retained

items.title: Construct control application services once in the composition root
items.story: Audit simplification: createJobManagementService and createSessionInteractionModule are rebuilt per route from global getters. Build them once in the control-server composition root and pass through ControlRouteContext; keep live settings getters behind explicit getters so settings-reload behavior is unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: job and session services constructed once and injected via ControlRouteContext
live values (settings reload, app identity, credential broker, clocks, event exchange) still read live
route tests pass before and after settings reload and runtime-store init

items.title: Collapse the direct-LLM loopback HTTP hop into one in-process service
items.story: Audit simplification (pairs with SEC-3): the direct API path serializes and buffers the same request through two in-process HTTP layers (public route -> loopback -> model gateway). Extract provider forwarding into one in-process application operation used directly by the public route; keep the loopback HTTP adapter only for child runners that need an HTTP endpoint. Do after SEC-3's concurrency admission lands.
acceptance_criteria: the direct API path no longer makes a loopback HTTP hop; one body reader and one serialize/deserialize cycle removed
provider credential isolation, per-app/route audit, provider path confinement, streaming, cancellation, rate limits, and child-runner gateway support unchanged
contract test: identical provider URL/headers/body/frames/usage/errors/cancellation before and after

items.title: Make durable coordination mandatory; remove process-local fallbacks
items.story: Audit simplification: AsyncTaskRepository atomic admission/claim are optional, and session compaction falls back to a process-global Set when the durable method is absent — two coordination systems with different guarantees. Make atomic admission/claim required for production repositories; the in-memory test repository implements the same atomic semantics. Removes createTaskWithLocalAdmission, optional-method probing, and the global compaction dedup set. Overlaps DUR-1 — fold in unless it lands sooner as standalone cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: atomic admission/claim required; local count/create and global compaction-dedup fallbacks removed
per-app/per-agent backlog caps, compaction dedup, stale-task recovery, fencing, and restart-safe records unchanged
concurrent admission tests pass against Postgres and the in-memory repository (two workers, restart, stale tasks, cap boundaries)
reconciled with DUR-1 (not implemented twice)

items.title: Architecture gate paydown — check:architecture exit 0
items.story: First story through the harness. Five failing sections inventoried in the goal-prompt: file-size splits (genai-spans worst), dangling doc refs, layer-import caps, provider-boundary tokens in tests, provider literals in prompt-profile-service.
acceptance_criteria: npm run check:architecture exits 0
python3 .agents/scripts/verify.py passes end to end
npm run typecheck and npm test green; no test deleted
no public API/contract change; contracts package diff empty
any remaining architecture exception carries an expiry/ratchet note

items.title: Permission engine: git deterministic-gate slice
items.story: Slice #1 of the hardened goal-prompt: deterministic rails precede any cache/classifier allow; git operations in trusted roots stop prompting.
acceptance_criteria: git clone/pull/fetch/status in trusted roots produce zero permission prompts in live runs
hard-deny/locked/fixed-image restrictions resolve BEFORE any cache or classifier allow
RunCommand telemetry records the command text (closes the RCA blind spot)
goal-prompt slice-1 acceptance list satisfied with tests pinning the ordering invariant

items.title: Permission engine: decision coordinator build (remainder)
items.story: Async host-side decision coordinator (cache -> classifier -> human) per the plan-validation-hardened goal-prompt; parse-fail and interpreter-with-string leaves always ask.
acceptance_criteria: single authority path: every lane reaches the coordinator via authenticated IPC on rule-miss
deterministic ask-floors re-run on every cache hit; never cached away
classifier verdicts cached with schema-enforced shape; ask-once behavior demonstrated live
goal-prompt acceptance criteria satisfied incl. its folded validation gaps

items.title: Observer: land staged S2 emission + S3a batch-core
items.story: Verify, one autoreview pass, and merge the rescued WIP branches feature/observer-s2-emission (e055dc14a) and feature/observer-s3-batch (a51a4909d) — the staged work was uncommitted tmp diffs until rescued 2026-07-22; exclude scratch docs from the review diff without deleting the design record.
acceptance_criteria: S2 floor (conf>=0.6, evidence>=1, dedup cosine>=0.86) merged behind observer.enabled default-off
S3a gateway batch endpoints + prefer-orphan state machine merged with its 7 autoreview fixes
API/SDK/E2E coverage per stage as in the goal-prompt
goals-index rows recomputed on close

items.title: Observer S3b: xAI Grok + Kimi batch transports
acceptance_criteria: both transports fill the same declared-capability slot as the Anthropic batch path
transport selection is config-driven; no provider literals outside adapters
hermetic tests cover both transports

items.title: Observer S4: digest delivery
acceptance_criteria: staging -> settlement -> freshness revalidation -> delivery pipeline per goal-prompt
evidence permalinks + feedback capture + delivery artifact recorded
at most one digest per day, owner-private routing enforced

items.title: Observer S5: setup wizard + preview + cold-start backfill
acceptance_criteria: gantry observer setup wizard step (extends apps/core/src/cli/setup-flow.ts)
preview + status surfaces work without enabling delivery
cold-start backfill bounded and idempotent

items.title: E2E: reconcile the test-matrix tracker
items.story: Matrix doc is behind reality (PRs #256-#261 added tests without flipping rows). Reconciling fires deferral D-0002 (persona/topology harness re-draft). Also reconcile feature/agent-e2e-haiku-turn: 15 commits, +382/-11 real diff vs main beyond merged #246 — salvage or discard with a citation.
acceptance_criteria: every matrix row states its true status with PR/test citations
gap list of genuinely-uncovered rows produced
D-0002 resolved: persona-harness scope re-drafted or explicitly re-deferred
feature/agent-e2e-haiku-turn dispositioned: salvaged into a row or discarded with rationale

items.title: E2E: remaining matrix rows
acceptance_criteria: packaged boot/restart, haiku model gate, all-tools coverage, security/recovery rows implemented per reconciled gap list
rows flip with citations per the standing merge-bar discipline

items.title: E2E: flip agent-e2e-gate to a required check
acceptance_criteria: agent-e2e-gate is a required status check on main
full matrix green at flip time

items.title: Close out the capability-authoring lane
items.story: Rescued WIP snapshot feature/capability-authoring @ 13ae2e698 (source of record). Imported WITHOUT a goal-prompt as a closeout exception (epics grill 2026-07-22): finishing in-flight work, not a new goal. Verify, one autoreview pass, merge; it edits mcp-tool-proxy.ts so nothing else may touch that file concurrently.
acceptance_criteria: branch verified (typecheck + unit + relevant integration) and reviewed via one autoreview pass
merged to main or explicitly split/reverted with a decision note
mcp-tool-proxy.ts conflict window closed

items.title: MCP hybrid search
acceptance_criteria: FTS ranking + light stemming on mcp_search_tools
opt-in semantic layer reusing memory embedding provider + cache, degrading to FTS
grill-locked goal-prompt acceptance list satisfied

items.title: Ponytail Phase-8 offline cutover
items.story: Phases 1-7 committed on feature/ponytail-audit through 9ffa146c2 (93-table baseline + Phase-8 runbook); post-Phase-7 in-flight diff rescued as e4104edc8. HARD GATE: executes ONLY on the user's explicit cutover go, behind a fully green ship gate + required e2e rows; any red = STOP.
acceptance_criteria: explicit user cutover go recorded before execution
fresh-green full gate + required e2e rows at cutover time
offline restamp/reset executed per the Phase-8 runbook with rollback verified available
post-restart runtime smoke green
lane merged to main

items.title: OTel permission/decision spans
items.story: Completes the observability taxonomy shipped in #220 (base) and #262 (tool-execution spans). Reconciled 2026-07-22: not started; no lane work exists.
acceptance_criteria: permission/decision spans per otel-permission-spans-goal-prompt.md
span taxonomy verified against the full agent/LLM/tool/MCP scope note on #262
hermetic tests cover the new spans

items.title: Durable-work primitive
items.story: Starts after the Ponytail cutover lands (it refactors jobs/interactions state the cutover moves). Fires deferral D-0001 (data retention) at start.
acceptance_criteria: goal-prompt acceptance list satisfied
D-0001 resolved: retention scoped into this lane or re-deferred with a new trigger

items.title: Model management: unify then UX
items.story: Starts after the Ponytail lane closes (shares the settings parser/renderer surface). Folds status-cost-cache-visibility UX remainder.
acceptance_criteria: model-management goal-prompt acceptance list satisfied
status/cost/cache UX remainder folded in or explicitly re-scoped

items.title: Jobs recovery-intent -> columns + CAS
acceptance_criteria: audit item B1 implemented: recovery intent as columns with compare-and-set transitions
no JSON-blob state machine remains for recovery intent

items.title: Coordination hardening batch (locks, TOCTOU, serializer)

each fix carries a regression test

items.title: desired-state-current-export rewrite

round-trip export/import test green

items.title: S3/MinIO file-artifact bytes
items.story: LOW PRIORITY (live uses local backend). Protocol decided: pending-row + upload + verified commit + TTL janitor. feature/s3-file-artifacts holds one [HOLD] stage-1 commit built commit-first BEFORE the protocol decision — rework to the decided protocol, do not merge as-is.
acceptance_criteria: goal-prompt protocol implemented with verified-commit semantics
TTL janitor covered by tests
local backend remains the default
